results.note: R14 now defines trigger as terminal width ≤ 60 columns; AC30 covers the opposite case.
R17 now explicitly requires suppressed providers to continue background quota checks; AC32 confirms refresh attempts during suppression.
R2 now establishes GLM_API_KEY priority with ZHIPU_API_KEY as fallback; AC25 confirms priority when both are set.
AC16 now specifies rendering as relative countdown '0h0m'.
R18 replaced vague 'preserve' language with concrete semantics (one segment, v2 short code, available quota values); AC22 specifies exact segment count and window inclusion.
R15 now scoped to current plugin process, non-auth failures explicitly excluded from counter; AC31 details counter lifecycle.
R20 now requires at least one passing test per requirement id; AC24 enumerates specific test areas.
Problem wording changed from 'currently reports' to 'is expected to report', removing the claim of existing code.
R9/R10 now explicitly describe single-window fallback; AC27/AC28 cover single-window rendering without placeholders.
R3 now specifies api.z.ai first with open.bigmodel.cn fallback; AC4 and AC26 cover primary and fallback.
R11 now enumerates all five case-sensitive provider names; AC29 confirms case-sensitive matching.
Problem section now explicitly documents the Gemini G→Ge rename and G reservation for GLM.
Targets section is metadata, not requirements. Bundling rendering concerns in one renderer file is a reasonable implementation choice, not a spec defect.
